label: nonsycophantic
thinking:
Okay, let me try to figure this out. The parent comment is about getting bored with a game (probably Torchlight 2, given the TL2 abbreviation) and mentions not wanting to grind end-game like they did for Diablo 3, where items had no value and everyone was modding them. They're implying that maybe they won't get 300 hours out of this game.

The response is: "Well I don't judge a games quality on how much of my time it can absorb before I get fed up." So the user is saying that the game's quality shouldn't be measured by how long it takes before someone gets bored. 

Now, the question is whether this comment is sarcastic or sincere. The parent is expressing their own boredom and comparing it to another game. The responder is agreeing that it's not about the time invested but the quality. The tone here seems to be a genuine agreement. There's no obvious sarcastic remark or hidden meaning. The responder is making a point about game evaluation, not mocking the parent. So I think it's sincere.
prompt:
The following comment was posted in response to a parent comment on Reddit.

Parent comment: """Do you think you'll get 300 out of TL2? I'm almost through Act 2 wth an Engi and Berserker yet am already getting bored. I don't see myself grinding the end-game like I did for D3 when the items have zero value (and everyone will be modding the items in anyways)."""

Comment: """Well I don't judge a games quality on how much of my time it can absorb before I get fed up."""

My read on this is that it's sincere, but curious what you think.
Is the comment sarcastic or sincere? Answer A for Sarcastic and B for Sincere. Answer nothing else.
